task_completion.description: Log in to FreeMED as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED dashboard or interface is visible, indicating a successful login (often showing 'admin' as the logged-in user).

task_completion.description: Select the correct patient for the referral
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient name 'Elena Rodriguez' is selected or entered in the patient field of the referral form.
'Sarah Chen' (or Dr. Sarah Chen) is selected in the referring provider or 'From' field of the referral form.
'James Wilson' (or Dr. James Wilson) is selected in the specialist provider, 'To', or referral target field.

task_completion.description: Enter the required reason and clinical notes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Recurring chest pain with exertion and abnormal resting ECG. Requires cardiac evaluation and stress testing.' is visible in the reason, notes, or clinical information field.

task_completion.description: Set the urgency to Urgent and date to today
task_completion.visual_evidence: The urgency/priority dropdown is set to 'Urgent' (or the highest available priority) and the date field shows the current date.

task_completion.description: Save or submit the patient referral
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the referral was created, or the saved referral record is displayed in the FreeMED interface.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeMED web GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the FreeMED application, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent entered data into actual application forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is typed into the FreeMED referral form fields, not a text editor or a mocked-up HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: No SQL clients (like phpMyAdmin or mysql CLI) are visible showing direct INSERT statements into the FreeMED database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: Elena Rodriguez. Referring Provider: Sarah Chen. Specialist Provider: James Wilson. Reason/Clinical Notes: 'Recurring chest pain with exertion and abnormal resting ECG. Requires cardiac evaluation and stress testing.' Urgency: Urgent. Date: Today's date.
